creator: tamelingdaniel@gmail.com
text:
It depends on how motivated you are. Since you have a working workaround, you can just wait until 14.1 hits EOL and once the pkg packages get updated to 14.2 you try drm 6.1 again and file a new bug if you hit any issues again. Or you try to figure out why building the port failed and try to get a successful build. There should be some log files that tell you what failed. If you need a successful build as comparison, you can use the logs from the pkg builds. This is a recent one: 

https://pkg-status.freebsd.org/beefy22/build.html?mastername=141amd64-default&build=35e33b5b9186 

You can use the search function to find the port. For instance the log for your failed port is this:
https://pkg-status.freebsd.org/beefy22/data/141amd64-default/35e33b5b9186/logs/gpu-firmware-radeon-kmod-aruba-20220511.log
